core_behavior: The system must handle HTTP requests to a specific API endpoint and return an HTTP 404 status code with a JSON error body when the requested resource does not exist.
The system will verify the existence of the requested resource and, upon determining its non-existence, generate an error response.
inputs: HTTP request method (e.g., GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
outputs: HTTP response status code: 404 Not Found
Other HTTP response headers as necessary (e.g., Cache-Control, Server)
JSON error body field error_code: a unique identifier for the error (e.g., "RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND")
JSON error body field message: a human-readable description of the error (e.g., "The requested resource does not exist")
assumptions: The system will use a case-sensitive comparison to determine the existence of the requested resource.
The system will use a predefined set of error codes, with "RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND" being the specific code for this scenario.
The system will not include any sensitive information (e.g., database query details, internal implementation specifics) in the error response.
The system will use UTF-8 encoding for the JSON error body.
failure_modes: If the system encounters an unexpected error while processing the request (e.g., database connection failure, internal server error), it will return a generic HTTP 500 Internal Server Error response with a JSON error body containing a unique error code and a human-readable message.
If the system is unable to generate a JSON error body (e.g., due to a serialization error), it will return a plain text error response with a generic error message.
edge_cases: Requesting a non-existent resource with an unsupported HTTP method (e.g., PATCH, OPTIONS): the system will return an HTTP 404 status code with a JSON error body.
Requesting a non-existent resource with a malformed URL: the system will return an HTTP 404 status code with a JSON error body.
Requesting a non-existent resource with an empty or null request body: the system will return an HTTP 404 status code with a JSON error body.
security_constraints: The system must not include any sensitive information in the error response that could be used to exploit the system or its underlying infrastructure.
The system must validate and sanitize all user input to prevent injection attacks or other security vulnerabilities.
The system must use secure communication protocols (e.g., HTTPS) to encrypt the error response and protect against eavesdropping or tampering.
performance_constraints: The system must respond to requests for non-existent resources within a reasonable time frame (e.g., < 500ms).
The system must be able to handle a high volume of requests for non-existent resources without significant performance degradation.
The system must use efficient data structures and algorithms to determine the existence of requested resources and generate error responses.
